commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds assistant-style chat error payloads from Gateway error broadcasts and teaches the Control UI chat controller to render active-run error events as visible assistant messages.

PR surface: Source +58, Tests +90. Total +148 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows chat `error` events only set `lastError`, and the PR body gives a Crabbox/WebVNC missing-auth repro; the review finding is also source-reproducible by sending a session-matching error event when `chatRunId` is initially null.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A single Control UI logic adjustment and regression test can address the blocking finding; because maintainer edits are disabled on the source branch, automation may need a narrow replacement branch if the contributor does not update it.

target.title: fix: show chat errors as visible messages
target.body:
## Summary
- Surface gateway chat error events as assistant messages.
- Render active-run errors in the Control UI chat while avoiding duplicate or orphan bubbles after failed sends.
- Add gateway and UI regression coverage for server-provided and synthesized error messages.

## Verification
- Crabbox aws/coral-krill run_3f3dd386ac44: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.error-broadcast.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts
- WebVNC aws/coral-krill: sent a Control UI chat message with missing openai-codex auth and confirmed the chat rendered the assistant error bubble.
- .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local: clean, patch is correct 0.84.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Web chat no longer stays silent when an agent turn fails before reply, such as missing provider auth after a goal is active.
Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox cbx_9d3c32cd8795 / coral-krill running the Control UI against gateway profile goal-webvnc.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent a Control UI chat message while the gateway had no openai-codex auth configured, then ran the focused Vitest lane on Crabbox.
Evidence after fix: The Control UI rendered an assistant error bubble with the missing openai-codex auth message; gateway logs showed the matching provider auth failure.
Observed result after fix: User gets a visible chat error instead of a stuck or silent turn; duplicate and orphan error bubbles are suppressed for failed sends that were already handled locally.
What was not tested: Full CI matrix locally; PR CI covers the broader repo gates.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
